content:
# 1 Thessalonians Chapter 4 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 202-203 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1TH_004_page_001.jpg, 1TH_004_page_002.jpg

**4:1** Finally then, brothers, we ask you and we urge [you] in [the] Lord Yeshua, just as you received from us the how it is necessary [for] you to walk and to please God, just as also you are walking, that you abound more.

**4:2** For you know what instructions we gave to you through the Lord Yeshua.

**4:3** For this is [the] will of the God: the holiness of you, [that] you abstain from the fornication,

**4:4** [that] each of you know [how] to acquire the vessel of himself in holiness and honor,

**4:5** not in passion of desire, just as also the nations, the [ones] not knowing the God,

**4:6** [that there be] no transgressing and defrauding the brother of him in the matter, because [the] Lord [is an] avenger concerning all these [things], just as also we told you before and solemnly testified.

**4:7** For the God did not call us upon uncleanness but in holiness.

**4:8** Therefore the [one] rejecting [this], not a man does he reject but the God, the [one] also giving the Spirit of him, the Holy [One], into you.

**4:9** And concerning the brotherly love, you have no need [for us] to write to you, for you yourselves are God-taught to love one another;

**4:10** for indeed you do it toward all the brothers, the [ones] in the whole of Macedonia. And we urge you, brothers, to abound more,

**4:11** and to be ambitious to be quiet, and to attend to the [things] of your own, and to work with the hands of you, just as we instructed you,

**4:12** that you may walk properly toward the [ones] outside, and may have need of nothing.

**4:13** And we do not want you to be ignorant, brothers, concerning the [ones] having fallen asleep, that you may not grieve just as also the rest, the [ones] not having hope.

**4:14** For if we believe that Yeshua died and rose, so also the God will bring with him the [ones] having fallen asleep through the Yeshua.

**4:15** For this to you we say by [the] word of [the] Lord, that we, the [ones] living, the [ones] remaining unto the coming of the Lord, shall by no means precede the [ones] having fallen asleep.

**4:16** Because the Lord himself, with a command, with [the] voice of [the] archangel, and with [the] trumpet of God, shall descend from heaven, and the dead in Christos shall rise first;

**4:17** then we, the [ones] living, the [ones] remaining, together with them shall be caught up in [the] clouds for [the] meeting of the Lord in [the] air; and so always with [the] Lord we shall be.

**4:18** So then, comfort one another with these words.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **4:1** -- Sinaiticus reads the extended form with "we ask you and we urge" (ερωτωμεν υμας και παρακαλουμεν), including the conjunction και which some later manuscripts omit. The phrase "just as also you are walking" (καθως και περιπατειτε) is present in Sinaiticus; some witnesses omit it.

2. **4:3** -- Sinaiticus reads "the holiness of you" (ο αγιασμος υμων), the standard reading across all major witnesses.

3. **4:8** -- Sinaiticus reads "into you" (εις υμας) rather than "into us" (εις ημας) found in some later manuscripts (Majority Text tradition). This is a notable variant: the second person "you" has stronger early attestation.

4. **4:13** -- Sinaiticus begins a new section here with "we do not want you to be ignorant" (ου θελομεν υμας αγνοειν), a characteristic Pauline disclosure formula.

5. **4:14** -- Sinaiticus reads "through the Yeshua" (δια του Ιησου) — with the article before the name, a distinctive Sinaiticus feature. The phrase "the ones having fallen asleep through the Yeshua" connects "through Yeshua" with falling asleep (i.e., those who died in union with Yeshua), though some interpreters connect it with "will bring." The syntax is preserved literally here without resolution.

6. **4:17** -- Sinaiticus reads "for the meeting" (εις απαντησιν) — the noun απαντησις carried a technical sense in the Hellenistic world of an official civic delegation going out to meet a visiting dignitary, then escorting him back. The literal rendering "meeting" is preserved.

7. **General** -- Throughout this chapter, Sinaiticus uses nomina sacra: ΘΥ for Θεου (God), ΚΩ/ΚΥ for Κυριω/Κυριου (Lord), ΧΩ for Χριστω (Christos), ΙΥ for Ιησου (Yeshua), and ΠΝΑ for Πνευμα (Spirit). These are expanded in the translation.

-- EMET
